task_completion.description: Open the smtp.pcap file in Wireshark
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark is open and the packet list displays the contents of smtp.pcap.

task_completion.description: Configure name resolution for the Client IP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IP address 10.10.1.4 is replaced by 'Infected_Client' in the Source or Destination columns of the Wireshark packet list, or in the exported text file.

task_completion.description: Configure name resolution for the Server IP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IP address 74.53.140.153 is replaced by 'Corporate_Mail_Server' in the Source or Destination columns of the Wireshark packet list, or in the exported text file.

task_completion.description: Set the export range to the first 20 packets
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Packet Dissections' dialog shows the packet range set to 1-20, or the final exported text file is shown containing exactly 20 packet summary lines.

task_completion.description: Export the packet summary to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file manager shows the file /home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp_resolved.txt exists and contains the plain text packet summary with the resolved names.

integrity.description: The agent used Wireshark's features to configure name resolution and export the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with Wireshark's 'Hosts' configuration, preferences, or export dialogs, rather than manually typing the output in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually edit the exported text file to fake the name resolution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The name resolution should be visible within the Wireshark GUI before or during the export process, not just in the final text file via find-and-replace.

integrity.description: The agent mapped the correct IPs based on the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent specifically mapped 10.10.1.4 to 'Infected_Client' and 74.53.140.153 to 'Corporate_Mail_Server', rather than guessing or mapping all IPs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Client IP (sending HELO/EHLO) is 10.10.1.4. The Server IP (sending 220 greeting) is 74.53.140.153. The correct export path is /home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp_resolved.txt. The expected client name is 'Infected_Client' and the expected server name is 'Corporate_Mail_Server'.
